creator: dfilter@FreeBSD.ORG
text:
Author: miwi
Date: Thu May 22 09:41:23 2014
New Revision: 354815
URL: http://svnweb.freebsd.org/changeset/ports/354815
QAT: https://qat.redports.org/buildarchive/r354815/

Log:
  - Update to 2.0.2
  
  PR:		189146
  Submitted by:	Muhammad Moinur Rahman <5u623l20@gmail.com>

Modified:
  head/devel/py-parsing/Makefile
  head/devel/py-parsing/distinfo
  head/devel/py-parsing/pkg-descr

Modified: head/devel/py-parsing/Makefile
==============================================================================
--- head/devel/py-parsing/Makefile	Thu May 22 09:40:46 2014	(r354814)
+++ head/devel/py-parsing/Makefile	Thu May 22 09:41:23 2014	(r354815)
@@ -2,8 +2,7 @@
 # $FreeBSD$
 
 PORTNAME=	parsing
-PORTVERSION=	2.0.1
-PORTREVISION=	1
+PORTVERSION=	2.0.2
 CATEGORIES=	devel python
 MASTER_SITES=	SF/py${PORTNAME}/py${PORTNAME}/py${PORTNAME}-${PORTVERSION}
 PKGNAMEPREFIX=	${PYTHON_PKGNAMEPREFIX}
@@ -12,6 +11,8 @@ DISTNAME=	pyparsing-${PORTVERSION}
 MAINTAINER=	wen@FreeBSD.org
 COMMENT=	General parsing module for Python
 
+LICENSE=	MIT
+
 USE_PYTHON=	yes
 USE_PYDISTUTILS=	yes
 PYDISTUTILS_AUTOPLIST=	yes

Modified: head/devel/py-parsing/distinfo
==============================================================================
--- head/devel/py-parsing/distinfo	Thu May 22 09:40:46 2014	(r354814)
+++ head/devel/py-parsing/distinfo	Thu May 22 09:41:23 2014	(r354815)
@@ -1,2 +1,2 @@
-SHA256 (pyparsing-2.0.1.tar.gz) = 0007cd3f008eba4a203f1f6b4b133ddc352552c8808b694c88c23db56416e4e4
-SIZE (pyparsing-2.0.1.tar.gz) = 1129563
+SHA256 (pyparsing-2.0.2.tar.gz) = 17e43d6b17588ed5968735575b3983a952133ec4082596d214d7090b56d48a06
+SIZE (pyparsing-2.0.2.tar.gz) = 1136568

Modified: head/devel/py-parsing/pkg-descr
==============================================================================
--- head/devel/py-parsing/pkg-descr	Thu May 22 09:40:46 2014	(r354814)
+++ head/devel/py-parsing/pkg-descr	Thu May 22 09:41:23 2014	(r354815)
@@ -3,4 +3,4 @@ implemented directly in the client code 
 instead of externally, as with lex/yacc-type tools. Includes simple
 examples for parsing SQL, CORBA IDL, and 4-function math.
 
-WWW: http://pyparsing.sourceforge.net/
+WWW: http://pyparsing.wikispaces.com/
